description: Dual-threshold configuration for performance gating. Warning thresholds produce alerts but do not block PR merges. Blocking thresholds prevent PR merges when exceeded. For higher_is_better metrics, negative percentages indicate a regression (decrease). Platform-specific entries take precedence; the 'default' entry is the fallback for platforms without an explicit section.

p50_ms.rationale: P50 latency is the primary comparison metric; 15% warns early, 30% blocks significant regressions

p95_ms.rationale: P95 tail latency has higher natural variance than P50; wider thresholds reduce false positives

p99_ms.rationale: P99 tail latency has the highest variance; matches P95 tolerance to avoid noise-driven blocks

peak_memory_bytes.rationale: Memory regressions are harder to reverse and compound over time; tighter thresholds catch them early

req_per_s.rationale: Throughput drop mirrors latency regression; negative values flag decreases as regressions

input_mb_per_s.rationale: Input throughput drop indicates processing efficiency regression; mirrors req_per_s thresholds

p50_ms.rationale: Medium tier P50 latency; same tolerance as small since P50 is stable across tiers

p95_ms.rationale: Medium tier P95 tail latency; wider tolerance for natural variance

p99_ms.rationale: Medium tier P99 tail latency; wider tolerance for natural variance

peak_memory_bytes.rationale: Memory regressions are harder to reverse; tighter thresholds than latency

input_mb_per_s.rationale: Input throughput drop indicates processing efficiency regression

p50_ms.rationale: Front-matter variant P50; same tolerance as medium since input size is comparable

p95_ms.rationale: Front-matter variant P95 tail latency; wider tolerance for natural variance

p99_ms.rationale: Front-matter variant P99 tail latency; wider tolerance for natural variance

peak_memory_bytes.rationale: Memory regressions are harder to reverse; tighter thresholds than latency

input_mb_per_s.rationale: Input throughput drop indicates processing efficiency regression

p50_ms.rationale: Large tier P50 latency; same relative tolerance since comparison is percentage-based

p95_ms.rationale: Large tier P95 tail latency; wider tolerance for natural variance

p99_ms.rationale: Large tier P99 tail latency; wider tolerance for natural variance

peak_memory_bytes.rationale: Memory regressions are harder to reverse; especially important for large inputs where memory usage is significant

input_mb_per_s.rationale: Input throughput drop indicates processing efficiency regression
